query: You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository. The codebase is at version 2.186 (commit: d6dd8b51). ## Issue to Resolve [BUG]: Can't display images with `#` in filenames ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? 1. Add a card including an image file containing `#` (hashtag) in its filename --- --- --- --- 3. Show the card in reviewer or previewer ### Expected behaviour The image is shown, as on 2.16.5. (2.16.5) ### Actual behaviour The broken-image icon is shown, and the image isn't shown. ### Debug info ```text AnkiDroid Version = 2.17.6 (568538896be943818ca793dd2635822265712def) Backend Version = 0.1.34-anki23.12.1 (23.12.1 1a1d4d5419c6b57ef3baf99c9d2d9cf85d36ae0a) Android Version = 11 (SDK 30) ProductFlavor = play Manufacturer = OPPO Model = CPH2013 Hardware = qcom Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 11; CPH2013 Build/RKQ1.200903.002; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/122.0.6261.119 Mobile Safari/537.36 ACRA UUID = df169a4d-29ec-46b6-b114-141ca7e01ba3 Crash Reports Enabled = true ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? - Originally reported in Anki Forum: https://forums.ankiweb.net/t/ankiweb-and-ankidroid-do-not-display-images-containing-pound-hashtag-sharp-symbol/42444/1 and dae has commented there. - As reported there, this issue occurs on AnkiWeb, too. ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/10436072/c588f585-b23c-4be6-b8f1-8a098a127705) So, strictly speaking, this is not a bug specific to AnkiDroid, but some approach specific to AnkiDroid may be needed, I'm not sure though. - This issue doesn't occur on Anki Desktop (as reported in Anki Forum) and on AnkiMobile. - The `#` is shown as `%23` on the editor of Anki Desktop (as reported in Anki Forum). ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/10436072/5ba75763-6007-4668-b036-0cb13e54cdc5) > \> Windows changes it back to a # in the sort field, but the HTML text remains unchanged > > This happens because Anki stores filenames without encoding on disk (legacy reasons), but needs to encode them before they’re shown in the editor. (dae) ### Research - [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app) - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [X] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) (2.18alpha6) ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion It seems to have already been fixed in the current HEAD. I don't think so, it is ongoing discussion in the PR, https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/15909#issuecomment-2001940011 Thank you for the feedback. Not for objection (since I don't have enough knowledge about the topic), just for rewording more accurately and for what it is worth, the issue seems to have been fixed in my debug version build from a854e93 (Mar 17, 2024). On the other hand, a card with the image file added in Anki Desktop is not shown properly (i.e. shown as a broken-image icon) even in the debug version of AnkiDroid. In this case, `#` is shown as it is in the editor and the browser. “#” is a illegal char in HTML, so it’s not supposed to work anywhere if not encoded. Ankidroid encodes it when importing, and AFAIK Anki does it as well. > Ankidroid encodes it when importing In case that users import cards that contain image files with unencoded `#`, for example, importing the following shared deck, why images with `#` in filenames are shown in reviewer on 2.16.5, but not on 2.17.6? - https://ankiweb.net/shared/info/789434294 --- Read now damien's comment about "legacy reasons". Since `#` still isn't supposed to be treated as something else that isn't a URL fragment marker in that context, this is a deliberate anki breakage of the URL scheme. I hate breaking things deliberately without a unavoidable reason, but I'll send the fix ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `.idea/dictionaries/davidallison.xml` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing. **If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files ## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS **NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

Called TemplateManager for technical reasons (conflict with Kotlin typealias) 51→ * 52→ * This file contains the Kotlin portion of the template rendering code. 53→ * Templates can have filters applied to field replacements. 54→ * 55→ * The Rust template rendering code will apply any built in filters, and stop at the first 56→ * unrecognized filter. The remaining filters are returned to Kotlin, and applied using the hook system. 57→ * 58→ * For example, {{myfilter:hint:text:Field}} will apply the built in text and hint filters, 59→ * and then attempt to apply myfilter. If no add-ons have provided the filter, 60→ * the filter is skipped. 61→ */ 62→class TemplateManager { 63→ data class TemplateReplacement(val fieldName: String, var currentText: String, val filters: List) 64→ data class PartiallyRenderedCard(val qnodes: TemplateReplacementList, val anodes: TemplateReplacementList) { 65→ companion object { 66→ fun fromProto(out: anki.card_rendering.RenderCardResponse): PartiallyRenderedCard { 67→ val qnodes = nodesFromProto(out.questionNodesList) 68→ val anodes = nodesFromProto(out.answerNodesList) 69→ 70→ return PartiallyRenderedCard(qnodes, anodes) 71→ } 72→ 73→ fun nodesFromProto(nodes: List): TemplateReplacementList { 74→ val results: TemplateReplacementList = mutableListOf() 75→ for (node in nodes) { 76→ if (node.valueCase == anki.card_rendering.RenderedTemplateNode.ValueCase.TEXT) { 77→ results.append(Pair(node.text, null)) 78→ } else { 79→ results.append( 80→ Pair( 81→ null, 82→ TemplateReplacement( 83→ fieldName = node.replacement.fieldName, 84→ currentText = node.replacement.currentText, 85→ filters = node.replacement.filtersList 86→ ) 87→ ) 88→ ) 89→ } 90→ } 91→ 92→ return results 93→ } 94→ 95→ fun avTagToNative(tag: anki.card_rendering.AVTag): AvTag { 96→ val value = tag.valueCase 97→ return if (value == anki.card_rendering.AVTag.ValueCase.SOUND_OR_VIDEO) { 98→ SoundOrVideoTag(filename = tag.soundOrVideo) 99→ } else { 100→ TTSTag( 101→ fieldText = tag.tts.fieldText, 102→ lang = tag.tts.lang, 103→ voices = tag.tts.voicesList, 104→ otherArgs = tag.tts.otherArgsList, 105→ // The backend currently sends speed = 1, even when undefined. 106→ // We agreed that '1' should be classed as 'use system' and ignored 107→ // https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/issues/15598#issuecomment-1953653639 108→ speed = tag.tts.speed.let { if (it == 1f) null else it } 109→ ) 110→ } 111→ } 112→ 113→ fun avTagsToNative(tags: List): List { 114→ return tags.map { avTagToNative(it) }.toList() 115→ } 116→ } 117→ } 118→ 119→ /** 120→ * Holds information for the duration of one card render. 121→ * This may fetch information lazily in the future, so please avoid 122→ * using the _private fields directly. 123→ */ 124→ class TemplateRenderContext( 125→ card: Card, 126→ note: Note, 127→ browser: Boolean = false, 128→ notetype: NotetypeJson? = null, 129→ template: JSONObject? = null, 130→ private var fillEmpty: Boolean = false 131→ ) { 132→ private var _card: Card = card 133→ private var _note: Note = note 134→ private var _browser: Boolean = browser 135→ private var _template: JSONObject? = template 136→ 137→ private var noteType: NotetypeJson = notetype ?: note.notetype 138→ 139→ companion object { 140→ fun fromExistingCard(col: Collection, card: Card, browser: Boolean): TemplateRenderContext { 141→ return TemplateRenderContext(card, card.note(col), browser) 142→ } 143→ 144→ fun fromCardLayout( 145→ note: Note, 146→ card: Card, 147→ notetype: NotetypeJson, 148→ template: JSONObject, 149→ fillEmpty: Boolean 150→ ): TemplateRenderContext { 151→ return TemplateRenderContext( 152→ card, 153→ note, 154→ notetype = notetype, 155→ template = template, 156→ fillEmpty = fillEmpty 157→ ) 158→ } 159→ } 160→ 161→ /** 162→ * Returns the card being rendered. 163→ * Be careful not to call .q() or .a() on the card, or you'll create an 164→ * infinite loop. 165→ */ 166→ fun card() = _card 167→ 168→ fun note() = _note 169→ fun noteType() = noteType 170→ 171→ @NeedsTest( 172→ "TTS tags `fieldText` is correctly extracted when sources are parsed to file scheme" 173→ ) 174→ fun render(col: Collection): TemplateRenderOutput { 175→ val partial: PartiallyRenderedCard 176→ try { 177→ partial = partiallyRender(col) 178→ } catch (e: BackendTemplateException) { 179→ return TemplateRenderOutput( 180→ questionText = e.localizedMessage ?: e.toString(), 181→ answerText = e.localizedMessage ?: e.toString(), 182→ questionAvTags = emptyList(), 183→ answerAvTags = emptyList() 184→ ) 185→ } 186→ 187→ val mediaDir = col.media.dir 188→ val qtext = parseVideos( 189→ applyCustomFilters(partial.qnodes, this, frontSide = null), 190→ mediaDir 191→ ) 192→ val qout = col.backend.extractAvTags(text = qtext, questionSide = true) 193→ var qoutText = parseSourcesToFileScheme(qout.text, mediaDir) 194→ 195→ val atext = parseVideos( 196→ applyCustomFilters(partial.anodes, this, frontSide = qout.text), 197→ mediaDir 198→ ) 199→ val aout = col.backend.extractAvTags(text = atext, questionSide = false) 200→ var aoutText = parseSourcesToFileScheme(aout.text, mediaDir) 201→ 202→ if (!_browser) { 203→ val svg = noteType.optBoolean("latexsvg", false) 204→ qoutText = LaTeX.mungeQA(qoutText, col, svg) 205→ aoutText = LaTeX.mungeQA(aoutText, col, svg) 206→ } 207→ 208→ return TemplateRenderOutput( 209→ questionText = qoutText, 210→ answerText = aoutText, 211→ questionAvTags = avTagsToNative(qout.avTagsList), 212→ answerAvTags = avTagsToNative(aout.avTagsList), 213→ css = noteType().getString("css") 214→ ) 215→ } 216→ 217→ fun partiallyRender(col: Collection): PartiallyRenderedCard { 218→ val proto = col.run { 219→ if (_template != null) { 220→ // card layout screen 221→ backend.renderUncommittedCardLegacy( 222→ _note.toBackendNote(), 223→ _card.ord, 224→ BackendUtils.to_json_bytes(_template!!.deepClone()), 225→ fillEmpty, 226→ true 227→ ) 228→ } else { 229→ // existing card (eg study mode) 230→ backend.renderExistingCard(_card.id, _browser, true) 231→ } 232→ } 233→ return PartiallyRenderedCard.fromProto(proto) 234→ } 235→ 236→ /** Stores the rendered templates and extracted AV tags. */ 237→ data class TemplateRenderOutput( 238→ var questionText: String, 239→ var answerText: String, 240→ val questionAvTags: List, 241→ val answerAvTags: List, 242→ val css: String = "" 243→ ) { 244→ 245→ fun questionAndStyle() = "$questionText" 246→ fun answerAndStyle() = "$answerText" 247→ } 248→ 249→ /** Complete rendering by applying any pending custom filters. */ 250→ fun applyCustomFilters( 251→ rendered: TemplateReplacementList, 252→ ctx: TemplateRenderContext, 253→ frontSide: String? 254→ ): String { 255→ // template already fully rendered? 256→ if (len(rendered) == 1 && rendered[0].first != null) { 257→ return rendered[0].first!! 258→ } 259→ 260→ var res = "" 261→ for (union in rendered) { 262→ if (union.first != null) { 263→ res += union.first!! 264→ } else { 265→ val node = union.second!! 266→ // do we need to inject in FrontSide? 267→ if (node.fieldName == "FrontSide" && frontSide != null) { 268→ node.currentText = frontSide 269→ } 270→ 271→ var fieldText = node.currentText 272→ for (filterName in node.filters) { 273→ fieldFilters[filterName]?.let { 274→ fieldText = it.apply(fieldText, node.fieldName, filterName, ctx) 275→ } 276→ } 277→ 278→ res += fieldText 279→ } 280→ } 281→ return res 282→ } 283→ } 284→ 285→ /** 286→ * Defines custom `{{filters:..}}` 287→ * 288→ * Custom filters can check `filterName` to decide whether it should modify 289→ * `fieldText` or not before returning it 290→ */ 291→ abstract class FieldFilter { 292→ abstract fun apply( 293→ fieldText: String, 294→ fieldName: String, 295→ filterName: String, 296→ ctx: TemplateRenderContext 297→ ): String 298→ } 299→ companion object { 300→ val fieldFilters: MutableMap = mutableMapOf() 301→ } 302→} 303→ 304→/** 305→ * The desktop version handles videos in an external player (mpv) 306→ * because of old webview codecs in python, and to allow extending the video player. 307→ * To simplify things and deliver a better result, 308→ * we use the webview player, like AnkiMobile does 309→ * 310→ * `file:///` is used to enable seeking the video 311→ */ 312→@NotInLibAnki 313→@VisibleForTesting 314→fun parseVideos(text: String, mediaDir: String): String { 315→ fun toVideoTag(path: String): String { 316→ val uri = getFileUri(path) 317→ return """""" 318→ } 319→ 320→ return SOUND_RE.replace(text) { match -> 321→ val fileName = match.groupValues[1] 322→ val extension = fileName.substringAfterLast(".", "") 323→ when (extension) { 324→ in VIDEO_ONLY_EXTENSIONS -> { 325→ val path = Paths.get(mediaDir, fileName).toString() 326→ toVideoTag(path) 327→ } 328→ in AUDIO_OR_VIDEO_EXTENSIONS -> { 329→ val file = File(mediaDir, fileName) 330→ if (isAudioFileInVideoContainer(file) == true) { 331→ match.value 332→ } else { 333→ toVideoTag(file.path) 334→ } 335→ } 336→ else -> match.value 337→ } 338→ } 339→} 340→ 341→/** 342→ * Parses the sources of the ``, `

content.thinking:
**Refining filename parsing for '#'**

The filename parser mishandles '#' by treating it as a URI fragment, cutting off the filename. The fix is to treat attribute values as raw filenames—encoding '#' as '%23' when building the file URI—and avoid losing fragments by not relying solely on URI parsing for paths without schemes.**Adjusting path construction to preserve '#'**

The existing URI parsing decodes percent-escapes but incorrectly splits filenames containing unencoded '#'. To fix this, I’ll detect if the attribute value has no scheme and contains '#'—then reconstruct the full path by combining decoded path and fragment or else treat the entire attrValue as a raw path, decoding percent-escapes but preserving '#' as literal. This ensures filenames match disk names despite '#' and encoding.

content.thinking:
**Clarifying invalid source handling**

The fix should allow parsing filenames with `#` but keep `%` sequences invalid, preserving existing test behavior without modifying test files. This requires tweaking parsing to accept `#` while still rejecting invalid percent-escapes as before.**Handling fragments in file paths**

The plan is to reconstruct file paths by appending the raw fragment (including empty ones) after the path when `#` is present, ensuring filenames with `#` are correctly processed without changing tests. Query parts will be ignored for now, focusing on fragment handling in parsing.

content.text:
Summary:
- Updated `parseSourcesToFileScheme` to preserve `#` in media filenames by reconstructing the full path (including fragments) before building the file URI.

Notes:
- This keeps existing invalid percent-escape handling intact while allowing filenames with `#` to resolve to local media files correctly.

PartiallyRenderedCard(qnodes, anodes) 71→ } 72→ 73→ fun nodesFromProto(nodes: List): TemplateReplacementList { 74→ val results: TemplateReplacementList = mutableListOf() 75→ for (node in nodes) { 76→ if (node.valueCase == anki.card_rendering.RenderedTemplateNode.ValueCase.TEXT) { 77→ results.append(Pair(node.text, null)) 78→ } else { 79→ results.append( 80→ Pair( 81→ null, 82→ TemplateReplacement( 83→ fieldName = node.replacement.fieldName, 84→ currentText = node.replacement.currentText, 85→ filters = node.replacement.filtersList 86→ ) 87→ ) 88→ ) 89→ } 90→ } 91→ 92→ return results 93→ } 94→ = tag.soundOrVideo) 99→ } else { 100→ TTSTag( 101→ fieldText = tag.tts.fieldText, 102→ lang = tag.tts.lang, 103→ voices = tag.tts.voicesList, 104→ otherArgs = tag.tts.otherArgsList, 105→ // The backend currently sends speed = 1, even when undefined. 106→ // We agreed that '1' should be classed as 'use system' and ignored 107→ // https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/issues/15598#issuecomment-1953653639 108→ speed = tag.tts.speed.let { if (it == 1f) null else it } 109→ ) 110→ } 111→ } 112→ 113→ fun avTagsToNative(tags:
